revision.text: #redirect[[en:Yuga_Daigongen]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]
#redirect[[由加山]]

'''瑜伽大権現'''（ゆがだいごんげん）は[[備前国]][[由加山|瑜伽山]]の[[山岳信仰]]と[[修験道]]が融合した[[神仏習合]]の神であり、[[阿弥陀如来]]・[[薬師如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。[[神仏分離]]・[[廃仏毀釈]]が行われる以前は、'''瑜伽山蓮台寺'''で祀られた。
== 由来 == 「此の山は無双の霊地にして、梵刹を開き、山密瑜伽の行を行い、我を瑜伽大権現として祀るべし」と夢のお告げを受けた[[行基]]が、[[天平]]５年に開山したとの伝承である<ref>蓮台寺縁起</ref>。行基が阿弥陀如来・薬師如来の二尊を祀ったのが瑜伽大権現信仰の始まりと伝わる<ref name='yugasan'>[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html 由加山蓮台寺 – ご縁起]</ref>。
秘仏の[[十一面観音|十一面観音菩薩]]を本尊として、一時は新熊野山蓮台寺と号した<ref>新熊野権現御伝記</ref>。
== 概要 == '''厄除けの権現さま'''といわれ[[池田継政]]以降の[[岡山藩]]主の篤い信仰を集めた一方で、[[瀬戸内海]]随一の信仰の聖地としても、[[金毘羅大権現]]と共に一生に一度は参詣して諸願成就を祈る'''両まいり'''の大権現として信仰された<ref name='yugasan'/>。
== 廃仏毀釈 == [[明治]]元年（1868年）の神仏分離令による廃仏毀釈によって、修験道に基づく瑜伽大権現は廃された。明治6年（1873年）瑜伽山蓮台寺は廃寺は免れたものの、[[真言宗]]の由加山[[蓮台寺]]と当時の[[国家神道]]の[[由加神社本宮|由加神社]]に強制的に分離された。
== 関連項目 == *[[由加山]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:ゆかたいこんけん}} [[Category:修験道]] [[Category:修験道の神]]

'''瑜伽大権現'''（ゆがだいごんげん）は[[備前国]][[由加山|瑜伽山]]の[[山岳信仰]]と[[修験道]]が融合した[[神仏習合]]の神であり、[[阿弥陀如来]]・[[薬師如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。[[神仏分離]]・[[廃仏毀釈]]が行われる以前は、'''瑜伽山蓮台寺'''で祀られた。
== 由来 == 「此の山は無双の霊地にして、梵刹を開き、山密瑜伽の行を行い、我を瑜伽大権現として祀るべし」と夢のお告げを受けた[[行基]]が、[[天平]]５年に開山したとの伝承である<ref>蓮台寺縁起</ref>。行基が阿弥陀如来・薬師如来の二尊を祀ったのが瑜伽大権現信仰の始まりと伝わる<ref name='yugasan'>[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html 由加山蓮台寺 – ご縁起]</ref>。
[[修験道|五流修験道]]の[[本山]]である[[五流尊瀧院]]の長床縁由興廃伝によれば、瑜伽山は'''新熊野那智山'''とされ、五流尊瀧院（本宮）・諸興寺（新宮）・瑜伽山蓮台寺（那智）を'''新熊野権現3社'''としていた。
== 概要 == '''厄除けの権現さま'''といわれ[[池田継政]]以降の[[岡山藩]]主の篤い信仰を集めた一方で、[[瀬戸内海]]随一の信仰の聖地としても、[[金毘羅大権現]]と共に一生に一度は参詣して諸願成就を祈る'''両まいり'''の大権現として信仰された<ref name='yugasan'/>。
== 廃仏毀釈 == [[明治]]元年（1868年）の神仏分離令による廃仏毀釈によって、修験道に基づく瑜伽大権現は廃された。明治6年（1873年）瑜伽山蓮台寺は廃寺は免れたものの、[[真言宗]]の由加山[[蓮台寺]]と当時の[[国家神道]]の[[由加神社本宮|由加神社]]に強制的に分離された。
== 関連項目 == *[[由加山]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:ゆかたいこんけん}} [[Category:修験道]] [[Category:修験道の神]]

== 由来 == 「此の山は無双の霊地にして、梵刹を開き、山密瑜伽の行を行い、我を瑜伽大権現として祀るべし」と夢のお告げを受けた[[行基]]が、[[天平]]5年に開山したとの伝承である<ref>蓮台寺縁起</ref>。行基が阿弥陀如来・薬師如来の二尊を祀ったのが瑜伽大権現信仰の始まりと伝わる<ref name='yugasan'>[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html 由加山蓮台寺 – ご縁起]</ref>。
[[修験道|五流修験道]]の[[本山]]である[[五流尊瀧院]]の長床縁由興廃伝によれば、瑜伽山は'''新熊野那智山'''とされ、五流尊瀧院（本宮）・諸興寺（新宮）・瑜伽山蓮台寺（那智）を'''新熊野権現3社'''としていた。
== 概要 == '''厄除けの権現さま'''といわれ[[池田継政]]以降の[[岡山藩]]主の篤い信仰を集めた一方で、[[瀬戸内海]]随一の信仰の聖地としても、[[金毘羅大権現]]と共に一生に一度は参詣して諸願成就を祈る'''両まいり'''の大権現として信仰された<ref name='yugasan'/>。
== 神仏分離・廃仏毀釈 == [[明治]]元年（1868年）の神仏分離令による廃仏毀釈によって、修験道に基づく瑜伽大権現は廃された。明治6年（1873年）瑜伽山蓮台寺は廃寺は免れたものの、[[真言宗]]の由加山[[蓮台寺]]と[[由加神社本宮|由加神社]]に強制的に分離された。
== 関連項目 == *[[由加山]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}

revision.comment: 以前の説明では、現在は蓮台寺で瑜伽大権現を祀っていないような物言いであったので、現在も尚祀り続けていることを加えた
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|神仏習合の神|その他の用法|瑜伽大権現 (曖昧さ回避)}} '''瑜伽大権現'''（ゆがだいごんげん）は[[備前国]][[由加山|瑜伽山]]の[[山岳信仰]]と[[修験道]]が融合した[[神仏習合]]の神であり、[[阿弥陀如来]]・[[薬師如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。[[神仏分離|明治初期、神仏分離]]令が発せられたときは「相応大菩薩」と名称を変え対応していたが、現在は瑜伽大権現に名称を戻し'''瑜伽山蓮台寺'''で祀られている。
== 由来 == 「此の山は無双の霊地にして、梵刹を開き、三密[[瑜伽]]の行を行い、我を瑜伽大権現として祀るべし」と夢のお告げを受けた[[行基]]が、[[天平]]5年に開山したとの伝承である<ref>蓮台寺縁起</ref>。行基が阿弥陀如来・薬師如来の二尊を祀ったのが瑜伽大権現信仰の始まりと伝わる<ref name='yugasan'>[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html 由加山蓮台寺 – ご縁起]</ref>。
[[修験道|五流修験道]]の[[本山]]である[[五流尊瀧院]]の長床縁由興廃伝によれば、瑜伽山は'''新熊野那智山'''とされ、五流尊瀧院（本宮）・諸興寺（新宮）・瑜伽山蓮台寺（那智）を'''新熊野権現3社'''としていた。
== 概要 == '''厄除けの権現さま'''といわれ[[池田継政]]以降の[[岡山藩]]主の篤い信仰を集めた一方で、[[瀬戸内海]]随一の信仰の聖地としても、[[金毘羅大権現]]と共に一生に一度は参詣して諸願成就を祈る'''両まいり'''の大権現として信仰された<ref name='yugasan'/>。
== 神仏分離・廃仏毀釈 == [[明治]]元年（1868年）の神仏分離令による廃仏毀釈によって、修験道に基づく瑜伽大権現は廃された。明治6年（1873年）瑜伽山蓮台寺は廃寺は免れたものの、[[真言宗]]の由加山[[蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|蓮台寺]]と[[由加神社本宮|由加神社]]に強制的に分離された。
== 関連項目 == *[[由加山]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}
{{Buddhism2}} {{神道 横}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:ゆかたいこんけん}} [[Category:権現]] [[Category:修験道の神]] [[Category:神仏習合]]

{{Otheruses|神仏習合の神|その他の用法|瑜伽大権現 (曖昧さ回避)}} '''瑜伽大権現'''（ゆがだいごんげん）は[[備前国]][[由加山|瑜伽山]]の[[山岳信仰]]と[[修験道]]が融合した[[神仏習合]]の神であり、[[阿弥陀如来]]・[[薬師如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。[[神仏分離|明治初期、神仏分離]]令が発せられたときは「相応大菩薩」と名称を変え対応していたが、現在は瑜伽大権現に名称を戻し'''瑜伽山蓮台寺'''で祀られている。
== 由来 == 「此の山は無双の霊地にして、梵刹を開き、三密[[瑜伽]]の行を行い、我を瑜伽大権現として祀るべし」と夢のお告げを受けた[[行基]]が、[[天平]]5年に開山したとの伝承である<ref>蓮台寺縁起</ref>。行基が阿弥陀如来・薬師如来の二尊を祀ったのが瑜伽大権現信仰の始まりと伝わる<ref name='yugasan'>[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html 由加山蓮台寺 – ご縁起]</ref>。
[[修験道|五流修験道]]の[[本山]]である[[五流尊瀧院]]の長床縁由興廃伝によれば、瑜伽山は'''新熊野那智山'''とされ、五流尊瀧院（本宮）・諸興寺（新宮）・瑜伽山蓮台寺（那智）を'''新熊野権現3社'''としていた。
== 概要 == '''厄除けの権現さま'''といわれ[[池田継政]]以降の[[岡山藩]]主の篤い信仰を集めた一方で、[[瀬戸内海]]随一の信仰の聖地としても、[[金毘羅大権現]]と共に一生に一度は参詣して諸願成就を祈る'''両まいり'''の大権現として信仰された<ref name='yugasan'/>。
== 神仏分離・廃仏毀釈 == [[明治]]元年（1868年）の神仏分離令による廃仏毀釈によって、修験道に基づく瑜伽大権現は廃された。明治6年（1873年）瑜伽山蓮台寺は廃寺は免れたものの、[[真言宗]]の由加山[[蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|蓮台寺]]と[[由加神社本宮|由加神社]]に強制的に分離された。
== 関連項目 == *[[由加山]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}
{{Buddhism2}} {{神道 横}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:ゆかたいこんけん}} [[Category:権現]] [[Category:瑜伽信仰]] [[Category:修験道の神]] [[Category:神仏習合]]

{{Otheruses|神仏習合の神|その他の用法|瑜伽大権現 (曖昧さ回避)}} '''瑜伽大権現'''（ゆがだいごんげん）は[[備前国]][[由加山|瑜伽山]]の[[山岳信仰]]と[[修験道]]が融合した[[神仏習合]]の神であり、[[阿弥陀如来]]・[[薬師如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。[[神仏分離|明治初期、神仏分離]]令が発せられたときは'''「相応大菩薩」'''と名称を変え対応していたが、現在は瑜伽大権現に名称を戻し'''瑜伽山蓮台寺'''で祀られている。
== 由来 == 「此の山は無双の霊地にして、梵刹を開き、三密[[瑜伽]]の行を行い、我を瑜伽大権現として祀るべし」と夢のお告げを受けた[[行基]]が、[[天平]]5年に開山したとの伝承である<ref>蓮台寺縁起</ref>。行基が阿弥陀如来・薬師如来の二尊を祀ったのが瑜伽大権現信仰の始まりと伝わる<ref name='yugasan'>[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html 由加山蓮台寺 – ご縁起]</ref>。
[[修験道|五流修験道]]の[[本山]]である[[五流尊瀧院]]の長床縁由興廃伝によれば、瑜伽山は'''新熊野那智山'''とされ、五流尊瀧院（本宮）・諸興寺（新宮）・瑜伽山蓮台寺（那智）を'''新熊野権現3社'''としていた。

== 神仏分離・廃仏毀釈 == [[明治]]元年（1868年）の神仏分離令による廃仏毀釈によって、修験道に基づく瑜伽大権現は廃された。明治6年（1873年）瑜伽山蓮台寺は廃寺は免れたものの、[[真言宗]]の由加山[[蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|蓮台寺]]と[[由加神社本宮|由加神社]]に強制的に分離された。
== 関連項目 == *[[由加山]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Otheruses|神仏習合の神|その他の用法|瑜伽大権現 (曖昧さ回避)}} '''Yuga Daigongen''' (瑜伽大権現) is a syncretic deity of {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} and {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}} originating from {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}'s {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山}}. The deity's {{ill|honjibutsu|ja|本地仏}} are {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} and {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu..."

[[修験道|五流修験道]]の[[本山]]である[[五流尊瀧院]]の長床縁由興廃伝によれば、瑜伽山は'''新熊野那智山'''とされ、五流尊瀧院（本宮）・諸興寺（新宮）・瑜伽山蓮台寺（那智）を'''新熊野権現3社'''としていた。

== 神仏分離・廃仏毀釈 == [[明治]]元年（1868年）の神仏分離令による廃仏毀釈によって、修験道に基づく瑜伽大権現は廃された。明治6年（1873年）瑜伽山蓮台寺は廃寺は免れたものの、[[真言宗]]の由加山[[蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|蓮台寺]]と[[由加神社本宮|由加神社]]に強制的に分離された。
== 関連項目 == *[[由加山]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}
{{Buddhism2}} {{神道 横}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:ゆかたいこんけん}} [[Category:Gongen]][[Category:権現]] [[Category:Gongen]][[Category:瑜伽信仰]] [[Category:Gongen]][[Category:修験道の神]] [[Category:Gongen]][[Category:神仏習合]]

{{Otheruses|神仏習合の神|その他の用法|瑜伽大権現 (曖昧さ回避)}} '''瑜伽大権現'''（ゆがだいごんげん）は[[備前国]][[由加山|瑜伽山]]の[[山岳信仰]]と[[修験道]]が融合した[[神仏習合]]の神であり、[[阿弥陀如来]]・[[薬師如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。[[神仏分離|明治初期、神仏分離]]令が発せられたときは'''「相応大菩薩」'''と名称を変え対応していたが、現在は瑜伽大権現に名称を戻し'''瑜伽山蓮台寺'''で祀られている。
== 由来 == 「此の山は無双の霊地にして、梵刹を開き、三密[[瑜伽]]の行を行い、我を瑜伽大権現として祀るべし」と夢のお告げを受けた[[行基]]が、[[天平]]5年に開山したとの伝承である<ref>蓮台寺縁起</ref>。行基が阿弥陀如来・薬師如来の二尊を祀ったのが瑜伽大権現信仰の始まりと伝わる<ref name='yugasan'>[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html 由加山蓮台寺 – ご縁起]</ref>。
[[修験道|五流修験道]]の[[本山]]である[[五流尊瀧院]]の長床縁由興廃伝によれば、瑜伽山は'''新熊野那智山'''とされ、五流尊瀧院（本宮）・諸興寺（新宮）・瑜伽山蓮台寺（那智）を'''新熊野権現3社'''としていた。
== 概要 == '''厄除けの権現さま'''といわれ[[池田継政]]以降の[[岡山藩]]主の篤い信仰を集めた一方で、[[瀬戸内海]]随一の信仰の聖地としても、[[金毘羅大権現]]と共に一生に一度は参詣して諸願成就を祈る'''両まいり'''の大権現として信仰された<ref name='yugasan'/>。
== 神仏分離・廃仏毀釈 == [[明治]]元年（1868年）の神仏分離令による廃仏毀釈によって、修験道に基づく瑜伽大権現は廃された。明治6年（1873年）瑜伽山蓮台寺は廃寺は免れたものの、[[真言宗]]の由加山[[蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|蓮台寺]]と[[由加神社本宮|由加神社]]に強制的に分離された。
== 関連項目 == *[[由加山]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] {{Infobox deity |associated_deities ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|qid=Q236242}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|qid=Q854773}}|attributes ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|qid=Q236242}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|qid=Q854773}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11573853]] Wikidata name: Yuga Daigongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Yuga Daigongen (disambiguation)}} '''Yuga Daigongen''' (瑜伽大権現) is a syncretic deity of [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]] and [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] originating from [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}. The deity's [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] are [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] order in the early Meiji period, the deity was renamed "Sōō Daibosatsu" (相応大菩薩) to comply with the new regulations but has since reverted to its original name and is enshrined at {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Origin == According to legend, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] received a revelation in a dream saying, "This mountain is a peerless sacred land. Establish a temple here, practice the three mysteries of {{ill|yoga|ja|瑜伽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoga|qq=|1=yoga in Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886203}}, and worship me as Yuga Daigongen." Following this revelation, Gyōki founded the temple on the mountain in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (733 CE). He enshrined Amida Nyorai and Yakushi Nyorai, marking the beginning of the Yuga Daigongen faith.<ref>Rendaiji Engi (Temple Record).</ref>
The faith further developed when Yuga Mountain was referred to as "New Kumano Nachisan," aligning with the {{ill|Five Schools of Shugendō|ja|五流修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Schools of Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to the records of the Five Streams’ Honzan-based {{ill|Goryū Sontakuin|ja|五流尊瀧院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryū Sontakuin|qq=}}, the mountain's temples—Rendaiji (Nachisan), Goryū Sontakuin (Hongū), and Shokōji (Shingū)—were collectively known as the "New Kumano Gongen Three Shrines."
== Overview == Yuga Daigongen is known as the "Deity of Disaster Prevention." The deity was deeply revered by successive lords of the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], starting with [[:en:Ikeda_Tsugumasa|Ikeda Tsugumasa]]. It also became one of the most significant pilgrimage destinations in the [[:en:Seto Inland Sea|Seto Inland Sea]] region. Alongside {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}, it was worshiped as one half of the "Dual Pilgrimage," with devotees visiting both shrines once in their lifetime to pray for the fulfillment of their wishes.<ref name="yugasan">[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html Yugasan Rendaiji – Historical Record]</ref>
== Shinbutsu Bunri and Haibutsu Kishaku == The [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] order of 1868, followed by the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement, led to the abolition of Yuga Daigongen as a Shugendō deity. Although {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} narrowly avoided closure in 1873, it was forcibly separated into the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]]-affiliated Rendaiji and the {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
{{Yuga Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|瑜伽大権現|version=75672952|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Alongside {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}, it was worshiped as one half of the "Dual Pilgrimage," with devotees visiting both shrines once in their lifetime to pray for the fulfillment of their wishes.<ref name="yugasan">[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html Yugasan Rendaiji – Historical Record]</ref>

Although {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} narrowly avoided closure in 1873, it was forcibly separated into the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]]-affiliated Rendaiji and the {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] {{Infobox deity |associated_deities ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|WD=Q236242}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|WD=Q854773}}|attributes ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|WD=Q236242}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|WD=Q854773}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11573853]] Wikidata name: Yuga Daigongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Yuga Daigongen (disambiguation)}} '''Yuga Daigongen''' (瑜伽大権現) is a syncretic deity of [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]] and [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] originating from [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}. The deity's [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] are [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] order in the early Meiji period, the deity was renamed "Sōō Daibosatsu" (相応大菩薩) to comply with the new regulations but has since reverted to its original name and is enshrined at {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Origin == According to legend, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] received a revelation in a dream saying, "This mountain is a peerless sacred land. Establish a temple here, practice the three mysteries of {{ill|yoga|ja|瑜伽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoga|1=yoga in Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886203}}, and worship me as Yuga Daigongen." Following this revelation, Gyōki founded the temple on the mountain in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (733 CE). He enshrined Amida Nyorai and Yakushi Nyorai, marking the beginning of the Yuga Daigongen faith.<ref>Rendaiji Engi (Temple Record).</ref>
The faith further developed when Yuga Mountain was referred to as "New Kumano Nachisan," aligning with the {{ill|Five Schools of Shugendō|ja|五流修験道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Schools of Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to the records of the Five Streams’ Honzan-based {{ill|Goryū Sontakuin|ja|五流尊瀧院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryū Sontakuin|qq=}}, the mountain's temples—Rendaiji (Nachisan), Goryū Sontakuin (Hongū), and Shokōji (Shingū)—were collectively known as the "New Kumano Gongen Three Shrines."
== Overview == Yuga Daigongen is known as the "Deity of Disaster Prevention." The deity was deeply revered by successive lords of the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], starting with [[:en:Ikeda_Tsugumasa|Ikeda Tsugumasa]]. It also became one of the most significant pilgrimage destinations in the [[:en:Seto Inland Sea|Seto Inland Sea]] region. Alongside {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}, it was worshiped as one half of the "Dual Pilgrimage," with devotees visiting both shrines once in their lifetime to pray for the fulfillment of their wishes.<ref name="yugasan">[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html Yugasan Rendaiji – Historical Record]</ref>
== Shinbutsu Bunri and Haibutsu Kishaku == The [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] order of 1868, followed by the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement, led to the abolition of Yuga Daigongen as a Shugendō deity. Although {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} narrowly avoided closure in 1873, it was forcibly separated into the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]]-affiliated Rendaiji and the {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
{{Yuga Faith}}

[[ja:瑜伽大権現]]
<!--Yuga Daigongen--><!--Yuga Daigongen--><!--Yuga Daigongen--><!--Yuga Daigongen--><!--Yuga Daigongen--><!--Yuga Daigongen--><!--Yuga Daigongen--><!--Yuga Daigongen--><!--Yuga Daigongen--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11573853}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] {{Infobox deity |associated_deities ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|WD=Q236242}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|WD=Q854773}}|attributes ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|WD=Q236242}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|WD=Q854773}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11573853]] Wikidata name: Yuga Daigongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Yuga Daigongen (disambiguation)}} '''Yuga Daigongen''' (瑜伽大権現) is a syncretic deity of [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]] and [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] originating from [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}. The deity's [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] are [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] order in the early Meiji period, the deity was renamed "Sōō Daibosatsu" (相応大菩薩) to comply with the new regulations but has since reverted to its original name and is enshrined at {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Origin == According to legend, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] received a revelation in a dream saying, "This mountain is a peerless sacred land. Establish a temple here, practice the three mysteries of {{ill|yoga|ja|瑜伽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoga|1=yoga in Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886203}}, and worship me as Yuga Daigongen." Following this revelation, Gyōki founded the temple on the mountain in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (733 CE). He enshrined Amida Nyorai and Yakushi Nyorai, marking the beginning of the Yuga Daigongen faith.<ref>Rendaiji Engi (Temple Record).</ref>
The faith further developed when Yuga Mountain was referred to as "New Kumano Nachisan," aligning with the {{ill|Five Schools of Shugendō|ja|五流修験道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Schools of Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to the records of the Five Streams’ Honzan-based {{ill|Goryū Sontakuin|ja|五流尊瀧院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryū Sontakuin|qq=}}, the mountain's temples—Rendaiji (Nachisan), Goryū Sontakuin (Hongū), and Shokōji (Shingū)—were collectively known as the "New Kumano Gongen Three Shrines."
== Overview == Yuga Daigongen is known as the "Deity of Disaster Prevention." The deity was deeply revered by successive lords of the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], starting with [[:en:Ikeda_Tsugumasa|Ikeda Tsugumasa]]. It also became one of the most significant pilgrimage destinations in the [[:en:Seto Inland Sea|Seto Inland Sea]] region. Alongside {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}, it was worshiped as one half of the "Dual Pilgrimage," with devotees visiting both shrines once in their lifetime to pray for the fulfillment of their wishes.<ref name="yugasan">[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html Yugasan Rendaiji – Historical Record]</ref>
== Shinbutsu Bunri and Haibutsu Kishaku == The [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] order of 1868, followed by the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement, led to the abolition of Yuga Daigongen as a Shugendō deity. Although {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} narrowly avoided closure in 1873, it was forcibly separated into the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]]-affiliated Rendaiji and the {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
{{Yuga Faith}}

Alongside {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}, it was worshiped as one half of the "Dual Pilgrimage," with devotees visiting both shrines once in their lifetime to pray for the fulfillment of their wishes.<ref name="yugasan">[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html Yugasan Rendaiji – Historical Record]</ref>
== Shinbutsu Bunri and Haibutsu Kishaku == The [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] order of 1868, followed by the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement, led to the abolition of Yuga Daigongen as a Shugendō deity. Although {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} narrowly avoided closure in 1873, it was forcibly separated into the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]]-affiliated Rendaiji and the {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] {{Infobox deity |associated_deities ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|WD=Q236242}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|WD=Q854773}}|attributes ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|WD=Q236242}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|WD=Q854773}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11573853]] Wikidata name: Yuga Daigongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Yuga Daigongen (disambiguation)}} '''Yuga Daigongen''' (瑜伽大権現) is a syncretic deity of [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]] and [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] originating from [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}. The deity's [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] are [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] order in the early Meiji period, the deity was renamed "Sōō Daibosatsu" (相応大菩薩) to comply with the new regulations but has since reverted to its original name and is enshrined at {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Origin == According to legend, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] received a revelation in a dream saying, "This mountain is a peerless sacred land. Establish a temple here, practice the three mysteries of {{ill|yoga|ja|瑜伽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoga|1=yoga in Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886203}}, and worship me as Yuga Daigongen." Following this revelation, Gyōki founded the temple on the mountain in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (733 CE). He enshrined Amida Nyorai and Yakushi Nyorai, marking the beginning of the Yuga Daigongen faith.<ref>Rendaiji Engi (Temple Record).</ref>
The faith further developed when Yuga Mountain was referred to as "New Kumano Nachisan," aligning with the {{ill|Five Schools of Shugendō|ja|五流修験道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Schools of Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to the records of the Five Streams’ Honzan-based {{ill|Goryū Sontakuin|ja|五流尊瀧院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryū Sontakuin|qq=}}, the mountain's temples—Rendaiji (Nachisan), Goryū Sontakuin (Hongū), and Shokōji (Shingū)—were collectively known as the "New Kumano Gongen Three Shrines."
== Overview == Yuga Daigongen is known as the "Deity of Disaster Prevention." The deity was deeply revered by successive lords of the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], starting with [[:en:Ikeda_Tsugumasa|Ikeda Tsugumasa]]. It also became one of the most significant pilgrimage destinations in the [[:en:Seto Inland Sea|Seto Inland Sea]] region. Alongside {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}, it was worshiped as one half of the "Dual Pilgrimage," with devotees visiting both shrines once in their lifetime to pray for the fulfillment of their wishes.<ref name="yugasan">[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html Yugasan Rendaiji – Historical Record]</ref>
== Shinbutsu Bunri and Haibutsu Kishaku == The [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] order of 1868, followed by the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement, led to the abolition of Yuga Daigongen as a Shugendō deity. Although {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} narrowly avoided closure in 1873, it was forcibly separated into the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]]-affiliated Rendaiji and the {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}

== Origin == According to legend, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] received a revelation in a dream saying, "This mountain is a peerless sacred land. Establish a temple here, practice the three mysteries of {{ill|yoga|ja|瑜伽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoga|1=yoga in Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886203}}, and worship me as Yuga Daigongen." Following this revelation, Gyōki founded the temple on the mountain in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (733 CE). He enshrined Amida Nyorai and Yakushi Nyorai, marking the beginning of the Yuga Daigongen faith.<ref>Rendaiji Engi (Temple Record).</ref>
The faith further developed when Yuga Mountain was referred to as "New Kumano Nachisan," aligning with the {{ill|Five Schools of Shugendō|ja|五流修験道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Schools of Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to the records of the Five Streams’ Honzan-based {{ill|Goryū Sontakuin|ja|五流尊瀧院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryū Sontakuin|qq=}}, the mountain's temples—Rendaiji (Nachisan), Goryū Sontakuin (Hongū), and Shokōji (Shingū)—were collectively known as the "New Kumano Gongen Three Shrines."
== Overview == Yuga Daigongen is known as the "Deity of Disaster Prevention." The deity was deeply revered by successive lords of the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], starting with [[:en:Ikeda_Tsugumasa|Ikeda Tsugumasa]]. It also became one of the most significant pilgrimage destinations in the [[:en:Seto Inland Sea|Seto Inland Sea]] region. Alongside {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}, it was worshiped as one half of the "Dual Pilgrimage," with devotees visiting both shrines once in their lifetime to pray for the fulfillment of their wishes.<ref name="yugasan">[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html Yugasan Rendaiji – Historical Record]</ref>
== Shinbutsu Bunri and Haibutsu Kishaku == The [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] order of 1868, followed by the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement, led to the abolition of Yuga Daigongen as a Shugendō deity. Although {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} narrowly avoided closure in 1873, it was forcibly separated into the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]]-affiliated Rendaiji and the {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
{{Yuga Faith}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] {{Infobox deity |associated_deities ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|WD=Q236242}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|WD=Q854773}}|attributes ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|WD=Q236242}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|WD=Q854773}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11573853]] Wikidata name: Yuga Daigongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Yuga Daigongen (disambiguation)}} '''Yuga Daigongen''' (瑜伽大権現) is a syncretic deity of [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]] and [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] originating from [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}. The deity's [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] are [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] order in the early Meiji period, the deity was renamed "Sōō Daibosatsu" (相応大菩薩) to comply with the new regulations but has since reverted to its original name and is enshrined at {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Origin == According to legend, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] received a revelation in a dream saying, "This mountain is a peerless sacred land. Establish a temple here, practice the three mysteries of {{ill|yoga|ja|瑜伽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoga|1=yoga in Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886203}}, and worship me as Yuga Daigongen." Following this revelation, Gyōki founded the temple on the mountain in the fifth year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (733 CE). He enshrined Amida Nyorai and Yakushi Nyorai, marking the beginning of the Yuga Daigongen faith.<ref>Rendaiji Engi (Temple Record).</ref>
The faith further developed when Yuga Mountain was referred to as "New Kumano Nachisan," aligning with the {{ill|Five Schools of Shugendō|ja|五流修験道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Schools of Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to the records of the Five Streams’ Honzan-based {{ill|Goryū Sontakuin|ja|五流尊瀧院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryū Sontakuin|qq=}}, the mountain's temples—Rendaiji (Nachisan), Goryū Sontakuin (Hongū), and Shokōji (Shingū)—were collectively known as the "New Kumano Gongen Three Shrines."
== Overview == Yuga Daigongen is known as the "Deity of Disaster Prevention." The deity was deeply revered by successive lords of the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], starting with [[:en:Ikeda_Tsugumasa|Ikeda Tsugumasa]]. It also became one of the most significant pilgrimage destinations in the [[:en:Seto Inland Sea|Seto Inland Sea]] region. Alongside {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}, it was worshiped as one half of the "Dual Pilgrimage," with devotees visiting both shrines once in their lifetime to pray for the fulfillment of their wishes.<ref name="yugasan">[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html Yugasan Rendaiji – Historical Record]</ref>
== Shinbutsu Bunri and Haibutsu Kishaku == The [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] order of 1868, followed by the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement, led to the abolition of Yuga Daigongen as a Shugendō deity. Although {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} narrowly avoided closure in 1873, it was forcibly separated into the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]]-affiliated Rendaiji and the {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}

{{wikidata link|Q11573853}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gongen]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yuga faith]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:Wikidata has English label]]" to "[[Category:qq]]"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] {{Infobox deity |associated_deities ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|WD=Q236242}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|WD=Q854773}}|attributes ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|WD=Q236242}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|WD=Q854773}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11573853]] Wikidata name: Yuga Daigongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Yuga Daigongen (disambiguation)}} '''Yuga Daigongen''' (瑜伽大権現) is a syncretic deity of {{ill|Mountain worship|en|Mountain worship|WD=Q93697439|lt=mountain worship}} and {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō}} originating from {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}'s {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}. The deity's {{ill|Honji suijaku|en|Honji suijaku|WD=Q3080781|lt=Honji Buddha}} are {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai}} and {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} order in the early Meiji period, the deity was renamed "Sōō Daibosatsu" (相応大菩薩) to comply with the new regulations but has since reverted to its original name and is enshrined at {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Origin == According to legend, {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} received a revelation in a dream saying, "This mountain is a peerless sacred land. Establish a temple here, practice the three mysteries of {{ill|yoga|ja|瑜伽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoga|1=yoga in Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886203}}, and worship me as Yuga Daigongen." Following this revelation, Gyōki founded the temple on the mountain in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} (733 CE). He enshrined Amida Nyorai and Yakushi Nyorai, marking the beginning of the Yuga Daigongen faith.<ref>Rendaiji Engi (Temple Record).</ref>
The faith further developed when Yuga Mountain was referred to as "New Kumano Nachisan," aligning with the {{ill|Five Schools of Shugendō|ja|五流修験道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Schools of Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to the records of the Five Streams’ Honzan-based {{ill|Goryū Sontakuin|ja|五流尊瀧院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryū Sontakuin|qq=}}, the mountain's temples—Rendaiji (Nachisan), Goryū Sontakuin (Hongū), and Shokōji (Shingū)—were collectively known as the "New Kumano Gongen Three Shrines."
== Overview == Yuga Daigongen is known as the "Deity of Disaster Prevention." The deity was deeply revered by successive lords of the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain}}, starting with {{ill|Ikeda_Tsugumasa|en|Ikeda_Tsugumasa|WD=Q28131624|lt=Ikeda Tsugumasa}}. It also became one of the most significant pilgrimage destinations in the {{ill|Seto Inland Sea|en|Seto Inland Sea|WD=Q231312|lt=Seto Inland Sea}} region. Alongside {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}, it was worshiped as one half of the "Dual Pilgrimage," with devotees visiting both shrines once in their lifetime to pray for the fulfillment of their wishes.<ref name="yugasan">[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html Yugasan Rendaiji – Historical Record]</ref>
== Shinbutsu Bunri and Haibutsu Kishaku == The {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} order of 1868, followed by the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku}} movement, led to the abolition of Yuga Daigongen as a Shugendō deity. Although {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} narrowly avoided closure in 1873, it was forcibly separated into the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}}-affiliated Rendaiji and the {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
{{Yuga Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|瑜伽大権現|version=75672952|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:瑜伽大権現]]

Establish a temple here, practice the three mysteries of {{ill|yoga|ja|瑜伽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoga|1=yoga in Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886203}}, and worship me as Yuga Daigongen." The faith further developed when Yuga Mountain was referred to as "New Kumano Nachisan," aligning with the {{ill|Five Schools of Shugendō|ja|五流修験道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Schools of Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to the records of the Five Streams’ Honzan-based {{ill|Goryū Sontakuin|ja|五流尊瀧院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryū Sontakuin|qq=}}, the mountain's temples—Rendaiji (Nachisan), Goryū Sontakuin (Hongū), and Shokōji (Shingū)—were collectively known as the "New Kumano Gongen Three Shrines."

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瑜伽大権現]] {{Infobox deity |associated_deities ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|WD=Q236242|1=Amitābha}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|WD=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}|attributes ={{ill|Amitābha Buddha|WD=Q236242|1=Amitābha}}; {{ill|Bhaiṣajyaguru Buddha|WD=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}}} {{Otheruses|Yuga Daigongen (disambiguation)}} '''Yuga Daigongen''' (瑜伽大権現) is a syncretic deity of {{ill|Mountain worship|en|Mountain worship|WD=Q93697439|lt=mountain worship|1=Mountain worship}} and {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}} originating from {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province|1=Bizen Province}}'s {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}. The deity's {{ill|Honji suijaku|en|Honji suijaku|WD=Q3080781|lt=Honji Buddha|1=Honji suijaku}} are {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}} and {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} order in the early Meiji period, the deity was renamed "Sōō Daibosatsu" (相応大菩薩) to comply with the new regulations but has since reverted to its original name and is enshrined at {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Origin == According to legend, {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} received a revelation in a dream saying, "This mountain is a peerless sacred land. Establish a temple here, practice the three mysteries of {{ill|yoga|ja|瑜伽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoga|1=yoga in Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886203}}, and worship me as Yuga Daigongen." Following this revelation, Gyōki founded the temple on the mountain in the fifth year of {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} (733 CE). He enshrined Amida Nyorai and Yakushi Nyorai, marking the beginning of the Yuga Daigongen faith.<ref>Rendaiji Engi (Temple Record).</ref>
The faith further developed when Yuga Mountain was referred to as "New Kumano Nachisan," aligning with the {{ill|Five Schools of Shugendō|ja|五流修験道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Schools of Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to the records of the Five Streams’ Honzan-based {{ill|Goryū Sontakuin|ja|五流尊瀧院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryū Sontakuin|qq=}}, the mountain's temples—Rendaiji (Nachisan), Goryū Sontakuin (Hongū), and Shokōji (Shingū)—were collectively known as the "New Kumano Gongen Three Shrines."
== Overview == Yuga Daigongen is known as the "Deity of Disaster Prevention." The deity was deeply revered by successive lords of the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain|1=Okayama Domain}}, starting with {{ill|Ikeda_Tsugumasa|en|Ikeda_Tsugumasa|WD=Q28131624|lt=Ikeda Tsugumasa|1=Ikeda Tsugumasa}}. It also became one of the most significant pilgrimage destinations in the {{ill|Seto Inland Sea|en|Seto Inland Sea|WD=Q231312|lt=Seto Inland Sea|1=Seto Inland Sea}} region. Alongside {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}, it was worshiped as one half of the "Dual Pilgrimage," with devotees visiting both shrines once in their lifetime to pray for the fulfillment of their wishes.<ref name="yugasan">[http://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html Yugasan Rendaiji – Historical Record]</ref>
== Shinbutsu Bunri and Haibutsu Kishaku == The {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} order of 1868, followed by the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} movement, led to the abolition of Yuga Daigongen as a Shugendō deity. Although {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|瑜伽山蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} narrowly avoided closure in 1873, it was forcibly separated into the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}}-affiliated Rendaiji and the {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
{{Yuga Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|瑜伽大権現|version=75672952|comment=Imported full ja history}}
